customManagers.description: Track uv version in UV_VERSION CI variable as the SAME Docker image ref as the Dockerfile COPY --from line. Using identical datasource + depName means Renovate treats both occurrences as one dep, so they are structurally guaranteed to update atomically in a single PR regardless of release age or preset changes.
Track ruff version in RUFF_VERSION CI variable via PyPI.

packageRules.description: Automerge uv minor and patch updates. Both the Dockerfile COPY --from and the CI UV_VERSION resolve to the same ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv Docker dep (see customManagers above), so Renovate naturally bundles them into one PR without needing a groupName. Major updates require manual review.

Disable digest pinning for the UV_VERSION CI variable - it is a plain version string consumed by shell commands (uvx, pip install uv==${UV_VERSION}), not an image reference. Appending @sha256:... would break those commands. Digest pinning still applies to the Dockerfile COPY --from line via config:best-practices, which is where it actually belongs.
Automerge ruff updates - linting tool with no runtime impact.
Group Python image updates across Dockerfile and CI into one PR. allowedVersions below constrains these to 3.13.x patch updates only.
Pin Python to 3.13.x across Dockerfile and CI to stay in sync with distroless/python3-debian13 (Debian Trixie). Bump intentionally when moving to a new distroless base.
Automerge Docker patch updates in GitLab CI (e.g. Kaniko).
Automerge Docker patch updates in Dockerfile and Compose files.
Automerge distroless base image updates - no shell or userland, minimal risk. separateMajorMinor disabled because tags do not follow semver.
Wait 3 days after a PyPI release before raising a PR - avoids packages yanked shortly after publishing.
Group application Python dependency patch updates (from pyproject.toml) into one PR. Safe to automerge - scoped to uv manager only so uv/ruff tool pins are unaffected.

Group application Python dependency minor and major updates (from pyproject.toml) into one PR for manual review. croniter is the only runtime dep, so any non-patch bump should be reviewed before merging.
